text: Artificial intelligence is changing how we think about creativity. Today, AI can generate impressive paintings, compose original music, and even write poems or stories. Some people find these creations beautiful or moving, but others feel they lack something essential: human emotion and personal experience. While a machine can follow patterns and produce artwork in seconds, human creativity often comes from feelings, memories, and unique perspectives. AI can be a useful tool for artists and writers, helping them explore new ideas or speed up their work. However, many believe that true creativity—the kind that surprises and touches us deeply—still belongs to humans. The future may not be about machines replacing artists, but about humans and AI working together in new ways.
